hard lessons so I had experience to help people through hard times. And I see that as valuable to people now who lack experience. And also get taken advantage of quite frequently, right? Entrepreneurs, creators, artists. It's very, very easy to jump into the world and just be, you know, stripped down to nothing and said, and be forced to be just a cog in a machine. And I value free will and autonomy so much that I make it my goal to, in my business, to help others stay free.

Aaron Yeager, you know? That's what I'm fighting for. That's why I asked. So again, this is just more or less so I understand your lens of the world and business. So your FinTech internship that you're doing, is that Dropout Degens or is Dropout Degens your business?

And so they send us about three to five EV plays per day, which ends up being like 2,000 a day, or sorry, Every minute, sorry, three every five minutes, which is about 2,000 a day. Again, I'll give you, I'll give you a, I'll send you the links to give you the ability to research for yourself. Okay. Um, it's all, it's all free, right? No, no harm, no foul. It won't hurt my feelings if you're like, no, dude, I'm going to make my own decisions and do it differently. That's just what I do for a living, right? I say, hey, here's what I'd recommend. Here's why I recommend you do it with me. Here's what I can do. And here's the difference between having me do it versus if you have to go to Wells Fargo, Bank of America, wherever, no. Nine times out of 10, you're not even going to get a real banker anymore.

You're going to get a computer that says, how do you want us to help you? Well, by the time you get to somebody real, you're going to have to explain your business every single time. If I'm your banker, never. Right. I know your business. I know exactly what's going on. Right. If we're doing business together, there's no need for you to judge. justify to yourself while you're lendable. I can lend you money and I give you terms and you say, these are the acceptable ones that I'll sign, right?

So the beauty of it is, is it's all a la carte. It's all, you know, at your comfort level. I position it as, I just want to be the first guy you call when you're thinking about doing something. So I can say, that makes sense for your business. Take my advice, take it or leave it, right? And then here's the routes I would recommend you go. Here's why. Full authentic transparency, right? If I'm going to make money, I'm going to tell you, right?

And if I'm going to make ridiculous money off of something you do with me, maybe we just split the cost. Like maybe we split the reward, right? Because there's no, like sometimes I just, I get a killer bonus, right? Maybe you do a loan with me that is 1% higher than Wells Fargo, even though it's technically through Wells Fargo. Maybe instead of you choosing somebody else, we look at the math and we say, well, yeah, repayment wise, it'll cost you more, but I can pay you the difference.

See what I'm saying? Like sometimes it just makes sense to have a full conversation. And that's all I ask for is allow me the opportunity to say we can or can't help. Here's how or how not. And I'd recommend this route because I've worked with thousands of entrepreneurs and we've done it a million different ways.
